votes.reason: The Python code correctly implements the Coq specification, checking that all three lists have the same length and that each element in the result list is the absolute difference of the corresponding elements in the game and guess lists.

The Python code correctly implements the Coq specification. abs_diff computes the absolute difference identically. compare_spec checks that all three lists have equal length (matching the Coq pattern that requires all lists to be empty simultaneously or all have elements), and verifies that each result element equals the absolute difference of corresponding game and guess elements. The iterative Python approach produces the same boolean/truth judgment as the recursive Coq Prop for all concrete inputs.
